headline: Cruise stocks surge as oil slides 3% on U.S.-Iran deal hopes
summary: WTI crude is currently trading at $90.70 a barrel, down $3.19, or 3.40%, on the session, according to Investing.com data. Brent crude fell $2.67, or 2.68%, to $96.91 a barrel. The catalyst: Iranian state TV reported the existence of a draft deal with Washington that could ease the shipping crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, the critical chokepoint through which a significant share of global oil supply flows. Reuters reported the development, citing the Iranian state media account.

content.title: Cruise stocks surge as oil slides 3% on U.S.-Iran deal hopes
content.summary: WTI crude is currently trading at $90.70 a barrel, down $3.19, or 3.40%, on the session, according to Investing.com data. Brent crude fell $2.67, or 2.68%, to $96.91 a barrel. The catalyst: Iranian state TV reported the existence of a draft deal with Washington that could ease the shipping crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, the critical chokepoint through which a significant share of global oil supply flows. Reuters reported the development, citing the Iranian state media account.

headline: United and Other Airline Stocks Are Flying. There Are More Gains to Come.
summary: United, Delta and other airline stocks were flying early Wednesday as the sector finally put the Iran war in its wake.  The  exchange-traded fund, which tracks the performance of airline stocks, jumped 3.3% in early trading and was on track to close above its pre-war, Feb. 27 level for the first time since the conflict started.  United Airlines  was leading the sector higher, rising 6%, entering positive territory for the year and also on track for its first close above its Feb. 27 price since the war began.

content.title: United and Other Airline Stocks Are Flying. There Are More Gains to Come.
content.summary: United, Delta and other airline stocks were flying early Wednesday as the sector finally put the Iran war in its wake.  The  exchange-traded fund, which tracks the performance of airline stocks, jumped 3.3% in early trading and was on track to close above its pre-war, Feb. 27 level for the first time since the conflict started.  United Airlines  was leading the sector higher, rising 6%, entering positive territory for the year and also on track for its first close above its Feb. 27 price since the war began.

headline: Wall Street hangs near its records as oil prices ease
summary: U.S. stocks are hanging near their records Wednesday as oil prices fall and ease the pressure on households and businesses worldwide.  Stocks of companies with big fuel bills helped lead the way on hopes that lower oil prices will remove a big drag on their profits.  Delta Air Lines rose 4.1% and is on track to set an all-time high.

headline: NORWEGIAN CRUISE LINE® REVEALS GRAND OPENING DATE FOR GREAT TIDES WATERPARK AHEAD OF LAUNCHING DAY PASS SALES
summary: Norwegian Cruise Line® (NCL), the innovator in global cruise travel, today announced that the highly anticipated Great Tides Waterpark at Great Stirrup Cay, the Company's private island in the Bahamas, will take place on Sept. 4, 2026, during Norwegian Luna's seven-day Caribbean sailing.

headline: US stocks inch to more records after oil prices drop
summary: U.S. stocks inched to more records Wednesday after oil prices fell and eased the pressure on households and businesses worldwide.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 182 points, or 0.4%, and the Nasdaq composite gained 0.1% as both indexes also set records.  Stocks of companies with big fuel bills helped lead the way on hopes that lower oil prices will remove a big drag on their profits.
